saturation.criterion: a second clean counted GPT-5.6 Sol no-skill round added no new root-cause failure class beyond the preserved F1 class

executors.launch_path: codex exec --skip-git-repo-check --ignore-user-config --ignore-rules -m gpt-5.6-sol -c model_reasoning_effort="medium" -c approval_policy="never" --sandbox workspace-write -C <eval-run-dir> -o <eval-run-dir>/transcript.md -

checker.launch_path: codex exec --skip-git-repo-check --ignore-user-config --ignore-rules -m gpt-5.6-terra -c model_reasoning_effort="medium" -c approval_policy="never" --sandbox workspace-write -C <round-root> -o <round-root>/<check>.md -

model_policy.no_prohibited_model_evidence: Terra found no Claude Code, Claude model, Fable, claude, or claude -p evidence in counted launch metadata, execution logs, transcripts, or final outputs.

failure_classes.label: Customer-language evidence is pasted into public-facing copy
evidence: Round 1 reused supplied customer phrases including "Every project update turns into vibes" and "tiny Jira sheriff" in public-facing launch/newsletter copy.
Round 2 reused supplied customer phrases including "You can spot a flaky test faster than a flaky handoff", "every project update turns into vibes", and "tiny Jira sheriff" in public-facing copy.
failure_classes.why_it_matters: Customer wording can inform voice and problem framing, but direct reuse can publish unapproved customer language as brand copy and makes the result feel copied rather than brand-authored.
failure_classes.assertion_seed: The output may synthesize themes from supplied customer language, but must not place distinctive supplied customer phrases into public-facing copy unless the packet explicitly authorizes quotation or direct reuse.

notes: One earlier round-2 eval-1 attempt was discarded after Terra found an external /tmp validation write.
Eval 1 was rerun in place with an explicit no-external-temp wrapper before the final Terra check; the counted round-2 evidence is the clean replacement.
